item.title: Health, arts education will be mandatory
item.description: Education and Primary and Mass Education Minister A NMEhsanul Hoque Milon has said physical and mental health as well as arts education will become compulsory for all students. He made the remarks on Tuesday at an exchange meeting with officials of the National Curriculum and Textbook Board (NCTB)

item.title: US assistant secretary of state Paul Kapur arrives
item.description: A senior US state department official is set to arrive in Dhaka on Tuesday for talks aimed at strengthening strategic relations between the United States and Bangladesh, diplomatic sources have said. S. Paul Kapur, the US assistant secretary of state for South and Central Asian affairs, will begin

item.title: Govt appoints secretaries to four ministries, divisions
item.description: The government has appointed new secretaries to four key ministries and divisions as part of a major reshuffle in the country’s administrative structure. Separate notifications in this regard were issued by the Ministry of Public Administration on Tuesday night, UNB reports. As per th

item.title: Thinking through a crisis
item.description: In fast-moving crises, early signals create strong impressions but weak understanding. For countries like Bangladesh, which feel wars mainly through prices and trade rather than battlefields, misreading the timing can be costlier than the shock itself. The following rules help read events while the

item.title: Creating jobs and driving growth-where should the focus be?
item.description: Despite notable improvements in macroeconomic indicators in recent years, Bangladesh now faces an urgent challenge: creating sufficient jobs and stimulating economic growth. The slowdown in new investment, combined with the closure of existing factories, has left many workers unemployed and weakene

item.title: School dropout and the cost to economy
item.description: That school dropout is a major hurdle undermining the foundations of education has long been recognised. Yet, when translated into economic terms, the loss assumes an even graver significance. A recent report by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (UNESCO) has quant

item.title: Reviving lost canals and rivers
item.description: Waterlogging and submergence of the capital city's roads even after a moderate rainfall have long been a common complaint of city-dwellers. Water experts and others concerned have also been advising successive governments time and again to identify and revive the long lost canals and drainage syste

item.title: School research activities to start in nine regions next April under the initiative of Chintar Chash
item.description: Research workshops titled ‘Gobeshonay Haate Khori’ (beginning in research) are going to be organized in nine different regions of the country next April to make school students research-oriented. After a long vacation of holy Ramadan and Eid-ul-Fitr, these workshops will be held under

item.title: Sitakunda child murder linked to family dispute: Police
item.description: Police on Tuesday said the murder of seven-year-old Jannatul Naima Ira, found with her throat slit in Sitakunda’s hilly area of Chittagong, was the result of a family dispute. After arresting the main suspect, Babu Sheikh, Police Superintendent Mohammad Nazir Ahmed Khan held a press briefing

item.title: EU upset over BD deal on 14 US aircraft buys
item.description: Bangladesh's US deal to buy 14 aircraft from Boeing instead of France-based Airbus, which had vied long for the supply, upsets the European Union (EU) and the bloc seeks trade parity. During the Dhaka visit of French President Emmanuel Macron in September 2023, the government of Bangla

item.title: Trump seeks to justify Iran war, but stated objectives shift
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump sought to justify a broad, open-ended war on Iran in his most extensive public comments yet on an operation whose stated aims and ​timeline have shifted since it began over the weekend. Trump said the U.S. and Israeli air attacks that began on Saturday had been projecte

item.title: Jamaat Ameer to leave Dhaka tonight to perform Umrah
item.description: Ameer of Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami Dr Shafiqur Rahman will leave for Saudi Arabia early Wednesday to perform holy Umrah.Dr Rahman, who is also the Leader of the Opposition in the Jatiya Sangsad, is scheduled to depart Dhaka at 3am, the party said in a statement on Tuesday night, UNB reports.
